question: Define a `struct Book` with members `title` (`std::string`), `author` (`std::string`), and `publicationYear` (int). Write a program that declares an array of three `Book` structs, prompts the user to enter details for each book, and then prints the details of all books.

test_cases.input:
The Lord of the Rings
J.R.R. Tolkien
1954
The Hobbit
J.R.R. Tolkien
1937
The Silmarillion
J.R.R. Tolkien
1977
Unwritten Book
Unknown
2024
test_cases.expected:
Enter details for Book 1:
Title: Author: Publication Year: Enter details for Book 2:
Title: Author: Publication Year: Enter details for Book 3:
Title: Author: Publication Year: Enter details for Book 4:
Title: Author: Publication Year: 
--- Book Details ---
Book 1:
  Title: The Lord of the Rings
  Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
  Publication Year: 1954
Book 2:
  Title: The Hobbit
  Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
  Publication Year: 1937
Book 3:
  Title: The Silmarillion
  Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
  Publication Year: 1977
Book 4:
  Title: Unwritten Book
  Author: Unknown
  Publication Year: 2024

driver: // The student's code is a complete program that interacts with stdin/stdout. An external test runner would execute it.
errors: Array out-of-bounds access: The `library` array is declared to hold 3 `Book` objects (indices 0, 1, 2). However, the loops iterate from `i = 0` to `i <= 3`, attempting to access `library[3]` in both the input and output phases. This results in undefined behavior, which could lead to crashes or corrupted data.
